{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 85,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Weep holes missing or fully blocked at bottom course of masonry wall.",
    "Condition prevents drainage of moisture from behind the wall, leading to potential water damage, efflorescence, and freeze-thaw deterioration."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop further backfill or finish work until weeps are cleared or installed.",
    "Open or drill clean weep holes at specified spacing (typically 24-33 in. o.c.) immediately above flashing.",
    "Verify that flashing is properly placed and drained, and that mortar droppings are prevented from blocking the cavity."
  ],
  "rationale": "Blocked or missing weeps in a cavity wall defeat the drainage system, trap water, and can cause structural damage, mold, and deterioration. TMS 402 and BIA tech notes require functional weeps at the bottom course to complete the flashing and drainage assembly.",
  "references": [
    "TMS 402/602 Building Code Requirements for Masonry Structures (Section on drainage and flashing)",
    "BIA Tech Note 7A - Wall Weeps",
    "BIA Tech Note 21 - Flashing and Drainage Details"
  ]
}
